title: Retina and RPE/Choroid RNA Extraction Protocol
author: Chottiwatt Jittprasong

steps.title: Add 1000 µL of TRIzol per...
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL of TRIzol per retina and pipette up and down 30 times to homogenize the mixture. This step should be done in fume hood due to toxicity of TRIzol.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of Chloroform per...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of Chloroform per 1000 µL of TRIzol used for lysis and mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 12000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 12000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase containing the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase containing the RNA to a new tube. Avoid penetration of the interphase layer.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Isopropanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Isopropanol to the aqueous phase, per 1000 µL of TRIzol used.

steps.title: Add 4 µL of Glycoblue and mix well
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL of Glycoblue and mix well.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 30000 x g, 4...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 30000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes Blue pellet should be present at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 1000 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 1000 µL of On ice 75% ethanol per 1000 µL of TRIzol. Vortex and centrifuge at 7500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Air dry the RNA pellet for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Air dry the RNA pellet for 00:05:00

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 20-50 µL of RNase free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 20-50 µL of RNase free water. 40 µL of RNase free water is recommended.
